does: This paper introduces WEval, a fine-grained evaluation pipeline for writing reward models that moves beyond coarse-grained assessment by measuring performance across specific writing requirements and task categories. Alongside WEval, the authors propose WRL, a reinforcement learning training framework that trains more precise reward models by constructing positive and negative samples through selective instruction requirement dropping. The approach systematically evaluates reward model rankings against gold-standard rankings across multiple writing tasks, demonstrating substantial improvements in how well models adhere to fine-grained writing requirements compared to existing coarse-evaluation benchmarks and LLM-as-judge approaches.
connects: This work builds on growing research into improving language model generation quality beyond standard perplexity metrics, extending prior work on reward modeling for text generation and reinforcement learning from human feedback. It responds to limitations in existing evaluation frameworks that treat writing quality as monolithic rather than decomposable, and addresses shortcomings in training approaches like those using LLM-as-a-judge systems that lack precision. The paper connects to broader efforts in instruction-following and requirement adherence in language models, drawing on reinforcement learning techniques for generation tasks while incorporating insights from structured evaluation methodologies that track multiple, concrete writing objectives simultaneously.
enables: This framework enables future work in precise requirement-aware generation systems where models can be trained and evaluated against specific writing constraints rather than holistic quality judgments. It supports development of more nuanced reward signals that could improve controllable generation across diverse writing domains, from technical documentation to creative writing. The fine-grained decomposition approach points toward better alignment between model capabilities and user specifications, enabling applications requiring strict adherence to multiple simultaneous requirements. Additionally, the systematic evaluation pipeline could facilitate development of better training data collection methods and opens pathways for combining multiple specialized reward models that each target distinct writing dimensions.
